data.title: Energy Transfer signs preliminary deal to build proposed Louisiana LNG plant
data.description:
Energy Transfer (NYSE:ET) has signed a preliminary contract with a consortium led by KBR (KBR) and Technip Energies (OTCPK:THNPY) (OTCPK:THNPF) for the construction of its proposed Lake Charles LNG plant in Louisiana, according a filing with the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission on Thursday.

The contract is contingent on a final investment decision for the proposed 16.5M metric tons/year liquefied natural gas facility; financial terms were not disclosed.

Energy Transfer (ET) has been trying to develop Lake Charles LNG since 2015 but has been unable to secure sufficient customer commitments, and the project has missed two FID targets.

After the Department of Energy in 2023 denied Energy Transfer's (ET) request for a second extension to export to countries that do not have a free trade agreement with the U.S., the company filed for a new permit.

data.title: New York Community Bancorp's Flagstar Bank cuts 700 jobs
data.description:
• New York Community Bancorp's (NYSE:NYCB) bank subsidiary, Flagstar Bank, is reducing its workforce by 8%, affecting ~700 employees as the company executes its transformation plan and integrates three legacy banks.
  • The headcount reduction comes after the NYCB overhauled its management, received a $1B capital infusion by an investor group led by Steven Mnuchin, and took billions of dollars of charges to re-value legacy transactions.
  • "While these strategic actions involve difficult decisions, including impacts on jobs, we believe they are essential for strengthening our financial foundation and building a more agile, competitive company," said Chairman, President, and CEO Joseph Otting.
  • New York Community Bancorp's (NYCB) Flagstar Bank will further reduce its payroll by ~1,200 employees when the previously announced sale of its mortgage servicing and third-party origination business is sold to Mr. Cooper (COOP) in Q4 2024. Most of the affected employees will be offered the opportunity to transfer to the buyer.
  • NYCB stock dipped 1.7% in Friday premarket trading.

data.title: Ciscom names Michel Pepin as CEO
data.description:
• Ciscom (OTCQB:CISCF) said it has appointed Michel Pepin as CEO and president.
  • The company has also named Frank Linhart as its finance chief.
  • Pepin has been a part of the company since its inception four years ago, and has formerly served as the CFO.

data.title: United States Cellular to sell portion of retained spectrum licenses
data.description:
• United States Cellular (NYSE:USM) said it has entered into an agreement with Verizon Communications (VZ) to sell a portion of the company's retained spectrum licenses for $1B.
  • The firm also entered into agreements with two other mobile network operators for the sale of other selected spectrum licenses.
  • "We are pleased that significant value for a portion of the remaining licenses will be realized," said CEO, Laurent Therivel.
  • The firm will sell 663 million MHz POPs of its Cellular spectrum licenses, as well as 11 million MHz POPs of its AWS and 19 million MHz POPs of its PCS licenses to VZ.
  • The company will also sell a total of 12 million MHz POPs of its spectrum licenses across the CBRS, C-Band, and 700 MHz B/C Block bands to two additional mobile network operators.

data.title: American Express Q3 earnings beat as network volume growth continues
data.description:
American Express (NYSE:AXP) stock slipped 1.3% in Friday premarket trading after the company trimmed the top end of full-year revenue guidance. At the same time, it raised its earnings guidance after Q3 EPS handily beat the consensus estimate.

The credit card lender and network increased its 2024 EPS guidance to $13.75 - $14.05 from $13.30-$13.80 vs. consensus of $13.28. Revenue growth guidance was revised to ~9%, compared with its prior outlook of 9%-11%.

Q3 total network volume of $441.0B, exceeding the $440.5B Visible Alpha estimate, rose from $440.6B in Q2 and $420.2B in last year's Q3. Network volume rose 5% Y/Y in the quarter, accelerating from 3% growth in Q2. Total billed business increased 6% Y/Y to $387.3B, the same rate of growth as in Q2.

Q3 adjusted EPS of $3.49, beating the average analyst estimate of $3.29, was flat with the previous quarter and rose from $3.30 a year ago.

Q3 revenue of $16.64B, vs. the $16.65B consensus, climbed from $16.33B in Q2 and $15.38B in Q3 2023. The year-over-year gain was driven by higher net interest income supported by growth in loan volumes, stable growth in card member spending, and accelerated card fee revenue growth, the company said.

Net card fee revenue climbed to $2.17B in Q3 2024 from $2.06B in Q2 and $1.85B in last year's Q3.

"We continued to attract large numbers of new premium card members with 3.3M new card acquisitions, while maintaining our high retention rates, excellent credit performance, and expense discipline," said Chairman and CEO Stephen J. Squeri.

American Express (AXP) acquired 3.3M new cards during the quarter, vs. 3.3M in Q2 and 2.9M in last year's Q3. American Express (AXP) has been adding card benefits to attract Millennial and Gen-Z customers, who represent 80% of new accounts acquired on the U.S. consumer Gold Card and remain the company's fastest-growing consumer cohort in the U.S.

Q3 net interest income of $4.01B vs. $3.73 B in the prior quarter and $3.44B a year ago.

Provision for credit losses was $1.36B, vs. the Visible Alpha estimate of $1.34B, $1.27B in the previous quarter and $1.23B in the year-ago period.

Consolidated expenses were $12.1B, less than the $12.2B Visible Alpha estimate, compared with $11.3B in Q2 and $11.0B in last year's Q3.

Card member loans increased to $134.5B from $130.9B in the previous quarter and $118.0B a year ago. Net write-off rate (principal, interest, and fees) was 2.2% vs. 2.4% in Q2 and 2.0% in Q3 2023.

Conference call at 8:30 AM ET.

Earlier, American Express Non-GAAP EPS of $3.49 beats by $0.20, revenue of $16.64B in-line

data.title: Graphite One receives indication for up to $325M in project funding
data.description:
Graphite One (OTCQX:GPHOF) has received a non-binding letter of interest for up to $325M debt financing from the U.S. Export-Import Bank (EXIM) to support the firm’s advanced graphite material supply chain project in the U.S.

The Graphite One Project plan includes an advanced graphite material and battery anode manufacturing plant located in Warren, Ohio as well as a recycling facility to reclaim graphite and other battery materials, to be co-located at the Ohio site. The building of these facilities remains subject to financing.

The company intends to make a production decision on the Graphite One Project upon completion of its feasibility study in the first quarter of 2025.

EXIM letter of interest is non-binding and indicates potential financing could provide 15-year repayment tenor under the bank’s Make More In America initiative. The transaction may also be eligible for special consideration under the China and Transformational Exports Program.

The proposed funding commitment is conditional upon completing the application, due diligence and underwriting process and receiving all required approvals. The company expects to submit a formal application to EXIM in 2025.

data.title: Ally Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.95 beats by $0.43, revenue of $2.06B beats by $30M
data.description:
• Ally Financial press release (NYSE:ALLY): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.95 beats by $0.43.
  • Revenue of $2.06B (+1.0% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • • CET1 of 9.8% up 20 bps quarter over quarter; $4.2 billion of excess CET1 above minimum requirement of 7.1%
  • Net interest margin (“NIM”) of 3.22% decreased 2 bps year over year. Excluding Core OIDA, NIM of 3.25% was down 1 bp year overyear.
  • Provision for credit losses increased $137 million year over year to $645 million, reflecting higher net charge-offs and a 15 bpsincrease in the retail auto reserve rate in the third quarter.

data.title: Procter & Gamble in charts: Fabric and Home Care segment sales hit an all-time high in FQ1
Solar Alliance Energy says CEO Michael Clark to resign
data.description:
• Solar Alliance Energy (OTCPK:SAENF) said its CEO, Michael Clark, has resigned from his role, and the firm has appointed Brian Timmons as his successor.
  • Clark is leaving Solar Alliance to pursue other opportunities.
  • Timmons has over three decades of experience.

data.title: Tobacco majors propose $23.5B settlement deal for Canada litigation
data.description:
The Canadian subsidiaries of cigarette giants Philip Morris (NYSE:PM), British American Tobacco (NYSE:BTI) and Japan Tobacco (OTCPK:JAPAF) (OTCPK:JAPAY) have proposed a C$32.5B ($23.5B) comprehensive settlement deal to end all tobacco product-related claims and litigation in Canada.

The subsidiaries have been under a court-appointed mediation for a possible settlement since they filed for bankruptcy protection in 2019 after a Quebec court ordered around C$15B in damages in a class action suit.

The proposed settlement amount would be funded by an upfront payment equal to the companies’ cash and cash equivalents on hand in Canada, plus certain court deposits and annual payments based on a percentage of their after-tax profits from tobacco products.

The allocation of the C$32.5B between the companies in the proceedings remains unresolved.

Jacek Olczak, CEO of Philip Morris, said: “Although important issues with the plan remain to be resolved, we are hopeful that this legal process will soon conclude.”

If the plan is approved and implemented, the subsidiaries of Philip Morris (PM), British American Tobacco (BTI) and Japan Tobacco (OTCPK:JAPAF) would pay the aggregate amount into trusts for claimants, comprising two primary components.
